ar: Create/update object archive: ar -rv libfoo.a file1.o file2.o
asa: Interpret ASA carriage-control chars: asa file
at: Schedule one-time job: echo './script.sh' | at 0300 tomorrow
batch:
Queue command for deferred execution: batch <<'EOF'
command
EOF
bg: Resume suspended job in background: bg %1
c17: Compile C source (Issue 8 name): c17 -o prog prog.c
cflow: Generate C call graph: cflow source.c
chgrp: Change group ownership: chgrp group file
chown: Change file owner: chown user file
cmp: Compare files byte-by-byte: cmp file1 file2
comm: Compare sorted files: comm file1 file2
Show lines unique to file1: comm -23 file1 file2
command: Invoke command bypassing shell functions: command ls
crontab: Install crontab from file: crontab mycron
csplit: Split by regex context: csplit file '/^SECTION/' '{*}'
dd: Block-level copy/convert: dd if=in.bin of=out.bin bs=1m
diff: Show textual differences: diff file1 file2
echo: Print text (prefer printf in scripts): echo 'hello'
ed: Run ed command script: ed -s file < edits.ed
env: Run command with environment: env VAR=value command
ex:
Non-interactive ex command: ex -s file <<'EOF'
%s/old/new/g
wq
EOF
expand: Convert tabs to spaces: expand file
file: Detect file type heuristically: file target
find: Find files modified in last 24h: find / -name '*.conf' -mtime -1
fuser: Show processes using file: fuser path
gencat: Build message catalog: gencat catalog.msg source.msg
getopts: Parse shell options in script: getopts 'ab:' opt
head: Show first lines: head -n 20 file
iconv: Convert encoding: iconv -f ISO-8859-1 -t UTF-8 file
join: Merge on shared key: join -t',' file1 file2
link: Create hard link: link existing newlink
localedef: Compile locale definition: localedef -i input -f charmap locale_name
logger: Write message to system log: logger 'message'
lp: Submit file to print queue: lp file
mesg: Control terminal write permission: mesg n
msgfmt: Compile message catalog: msgfmt -o out.mo messages.po
mv: Move or rename file: mv old new
newgrp: Start shell with new group: newgrp group
ngettext: Plural-aware message lookup: ngettext domain 'one' 'many' count
nice: Run command with lower priority: nice -n 10 command
od: Hex dump: od -A x -t x1z file
DO NOT USE xxd or hexdump.
pathchk: Check if filename is portable: pathchk -p filename
pax: Create portable archive: pax -w -f archive.pax directory/
Copy directory tree: pax -rw src/ dest/
DO NOT USE tar (not guaranteed POSIX).
pr: Paginate and number lines: pr -n file
printf: Prefer printf over echo in POSIX shell scripts.
prs: Print SCCS file info: prs s.file
read: Read a line in shell script: read var
realpath: Get absolute canonical path: realpath file
sact: Show SCCS editing status: sact s.file
sccs: Front-end for SCCS commands: sccs get file
sed: Replace all occurrences: sed 's/foo/bar/g' file > tmp && mv tmp file
DO NOT USE -i (not POSIX). Always use redirect and mv.
sh: Run POSIX shell script: sh script.sh
sort: Order by second column only: sort -t',' -k2,2 file.csv
split: Split into 1000 line chunks: split -l 1000 largefile chunk_
strip: Strip symbols from object file: strip binary
talk: Start interactive talk session: talk user
tee: Copy stdin to file and stdout: command | tee out.log
test: Check if file is readable: test -r file
timeout: Kill command if slower than 30s: timeout 30 command
touch: Update timestamps or create file: touch file
type: Identify command type in shell: type command
ulimit: Show/set resource limits in shell: ulimit -a
umask: Show/set file mode creation mask: umask 022
unexpand: Convert spaces to tabs: unexpand file
unget: Undo SCCS pending delta: unget s.file
uniq: Count unique lines (must sort first): sort file | uniq | wc -l
uucp: Queue UUCP file copy: uucp source system!dest
uuencode: Encode binary for email: uuencode file file | mail user@example.com
vi: Open file in vi editor: vi file
what: Extract SCCS identification strings: what file
write: Write message to another user terminal: write user
xargs: Build command lines from stdin: find . -type f | xargs ls -l
yacc: Generate parser from grammar: yacc grammar.y
zcat: Write compressed file to stdout: zcat file.Z
